title: Compose V2: Cannot run docker-compose if a container uses `build:`
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

If any container has a `build:` directive, `docker-compose` fails to start.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```dockerfile
FROM alpine:latest

RUN echo 1
```

```yaml
services:
  test:
    build: .
    command: ls /app
```

**Describe the results you received:**


```console
$ docker-compose up
[+] Building 0.0s (0/0)
failed to get status: rpc error: code = Unavailable desc = connection error: desc = "transport: Error while dialing unable to upgrade to h2c, received 404"
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

This should build and run.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Works fine with docker-compose.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```console
$ podman --version
dpodman version 3.4.0
$ docker-compose --version
Docker Compose version 2.0.1
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


comments.body: Yes, docker-compose V2 does not work at the moment. I recommend to use the 1.X python based version for now.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Looks like this has been fixed in the meantime?

I don't get any errors when running the steps to reproduce:

```
docker-compose up                                                                                     
Sending build context to Docker daemon     221B
STEP 1/2: FROM alpine:latest
STEP 2/2: RUN echo 1
--> Using cache 1c551f141a7f83bb58b76fcd5f7295ae5742c1a5f66ebfa7352ba961581c35b0
COMMIT podman-test_test
--> 1c551f141a7
Successfully tagged localhost/podman-test_test:latest

1c551f141a7f83bb58b76fcd5f7295ae5742c1a5f66ebfa7352ba961581c35b0
Successfully built 1c551f141a7f
Successfully tagged podman-test_test
[+] Running 2/0

Attaching to podman-test-test-1
podman-test-test-1  | ls: /app: No such file or directory
```

podman version:

```
Version:      3.4.4
API Version:  3.4.4
Go Version:   go1.17.4
Git Commit:   f6526ada1025c2e3f88745ba83b8b461ca659933
Built:        Thu Dec  9 19:30:40 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

docker-compose version:

```
Docker Compose version 2.2.3
```
@heapifyman I tested with both podman 3.4.4 and  4.0.0 and it is not working with compose v2, did you change something?

@brnosouza I don't think so. I just copy pasted the Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml content from @WhyNotHugo and ran `docker-compose up`.

I am using Manjaro Linux. I have installed packages:

* podman
* podman-dnsname
* podman-docker
* docker-compose

from official Manjaro / Arch community repository.

In addition, I have the following in `~/.config/containers/registries.conf̀`:

```
unqualified-search-registries = ["docker.io"]
```

And the following environment variable - as described at https://wiki.archlinux.org/title/Podman#Docker_Compose:

```
DOCKER_HOST=unix:///run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock
```

Output of `podman info --debug̀` looks pretty much the same as the one posted by ̀@WhyNotHugo. Version numbers are of course different and I am on kernel `5.16.7-1-MANJARO`.

@heapifyman I tested using docker-compose v2.2.3 and podman 4, and the output was kinda the same:

```shell
$ docker-compose up

failed to get status: rpc error: code = Unavailable desc = connection error: desc = "transport: Error while dialing unable to upgrade to h2c, received 404"
```

I'm using Fedora 35 in this case 🤔

We've fixed the other notable Compose v2 issue, and added Compose v2.2.3 to our test suite (which includes several compose projects with builds), and it seems to be working fine so far. Given this, I'm going to close this, but if anyone is able to reproduce with Podman's main branch and Compose v2.2.3, I'd be very interested in seeing a reproducer.

For anyone else encountering this issue:
The resolution seems to currently be downgrading to `docker-compose` `v2.2.3`

On macOS the downgrade can be performed like this:
```bash
brew uninstall docker-compose
wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/homebrew-core/bf19d2715bbab845a440d411aa9281c776fa2fcf/Formula/docker-compose.rb
brew install docker-compose.rb
rm docker-compose.rb
brew pin docker-compose
```

As of the time of this writing the `"transport: Error while dialing unable to upgrade to h2c, received 404"` seems to affect most versions after `v2.2.3` up to and including `v2.4.1`

Can you open a fresh issue? I doubt that `docker compose` reverted their legacy build support. Possible they now default to Buildkit and it needs to be forced to use the standard build API? I can look into it.

> For anyone else encountering this issue: The resolution seems to currently be downgrading to `docker-compose` `v2.2.3`
> 
> On macOS the downgrade can be performed like this:
> 
> ```shell
> brew uninstall docker-compose
> wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/homebrew-core/bf19d2715bbab845a440d411aa9281c776fa2fcf/Formula/docker-compose.rb
> brew install docker-compose.rb
> rm docker-compose.rb
> brew pin docker-compose
> ```
> 
> As of the time of this writing the `"transport: Error while dialing unable to upgrade to h2c, received 404"` seems to affect most versions after `v2.2.3` up to and including `v2.4.1`

I used latest docker-compose version, currently it's `2.6.1`, on CentOS 7 production server and got this issue. Solved by downgrading straight to `2.2.2`
The preferred solution is to set an environment variable (`DOCKER_BUILDKIT=0`) to force use of the old build backend.
